# criblio_search_engine (Resource)

SearchEngine Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_search_engine" "my_searchengine" {
  description = "Primary ingestion engine for the default local Dataset."
  id          = "local_ingest_primary"
  tier_size   = "small"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) The <code>id</code> of the Local Search engine to update.
- `tier_size` (String) Storage and compute [tier size](https://docs.cribl.io/search/engine-setup/#sizes-list) for the lakehouse engine. Configuring sizes <code>3xlarge</code>, <code>4xlarge</code>,<code>5xlarge</code>, and <code>6xlarge</code> requires the <code>cribl_operator</code> role. possible known values include one of ["3xsmall", "2xsmall", "xsmall", "small", "medium", "large", "xlarge", "2xlarge", "3xlarge", "4xlarge", "5xlarge", "6xlarge"]

### Optional

- `description` (String) Brief description of the lakehouse engine.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_search_engine.my_criblio_search_engine
  id = "local_ingest_primary"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_search_engine.my_criblio_search_engine "local_ingest_primary"
```
